sections.title: Why Arrays matters
sections.content:
Arrays are one of the most common interview topics and the base of many data structure problems.

If your array fundamentals are weak, problems like sliding window, two pointers, and sorting become difficult.

In real products, arrays appear in data processing, report generation, caching snapshots, and algorithmic features. Correct indexing and bounds handling are critical to avoid runtime crashes.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Arrays
sections.content:
An array stores fixed-size elements of same type.

### Declaration
- 'int[] nums = new int[5];'
- 'int[] nums = {3, 1, 4, 1, 5};'

### Access
- Read: 'nums[i]'
- Write: 'nums[i] = value'

### Important properties
- Zero-based index
- Fixed length after creation
- Fast random access O(1)

### Common operations
- Traverse array
- Find max/min
- Reverse
- Sort using 'Arrays.sort()'

### 2D arrays
Represent table/matrix structures using 'int[][]'.

### Interview warning
Most array bugs come from invalid index and loop bounds.

### Satyverse — deeper path (theory → practice)
**Arrays are fixed-size; lists are the default API surface in apps.** Know when zero-based indexing bites (off-by-one in `substring`, loop `< length`).

**Multi-dimensional arrays:** in Java they are “arrays of arrays”; ragged rows are legal — useful in graph adjacency lists, wasteful if you assumed a matrix.

**`Arrays.*` utilities:** `copyOf`, `fill`, `sort`, `binarySearch` — learn one method deeply per week and use them in exercises instead of hand-rolling.

**Memory picture:** a large `byte[]` for I/O buffers; an `int[]` for competitive-style problems; `Object[]` for heterogeneous legacy — prefer generics collections for new code.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Fixed-size int array: length, indexing, and Arrays.sort — foundation for collections later.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Two-pointer in-place reverse — classic array technique used in interviews.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Kadane-style maximum subarray sum — O(n) scan over an int[] (arrays + algorithm).

sections.title: Architecture View - Day 4
Common Pitfalls
items: Accessing arr[arr.length] which is always invalid index.
Using <= instead of < in loop condition incorrectly.
Forgetting array is fixed size after creation.
Not handling empty array before arr[0] access.
Modifying array while assuming original order is preserved.

sections.title: Exercise - Implement Arrays
sections.problem:
Implement a Java program for **Arrays** with real input and edge-case handling.

Requirements:
1. Accept at least 5 sample cases.
2. Print clear intermediate output.
3. Include one invalid/boundary case.
4. Explain time complexity in comments.
5. Keep naming and structure interview-ready.
hints: Start with a straightforward solution first.
Write at least one edge case test.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is the length of an array after int[] a = new int[5]?
conceptual.answer: a.length is 5. Length is fixed at creation and cannot be resized.

conceptual.question: What is valid index range for an array of length n?
conceptual.answer: 0 through n-1 inclusive; index n is out of bounds.

conceptual.question: What is a jagged array in Java?
conceptual.answer: A 2D array where each row can have different lengths—an array of int[] rows.

conceptual.question: What does Arrays.sort do for int[] vs Object[]?
conceptual.answer: For primitives, uses tuned dual-pivot quicksort; for objects, uses TimSort and requires Comparable or Comparator.

conceptual.question: What is System.arraycopy used for?
conceptual.answer: Fast copying between arrays with specified source and destination offsets and length.

conceptual.question: Why might you prefer Arrays.copyOf over manual loops?
conceptual.answer: It is concise and can resize arrays (truncate or pad with defaults).

conceptual.question: What is the default value of new int[10] elements?
Can you resize an array in place?
conceptual.answer: No—you create a new array and copy elements, or use ArrayList for dynamic sizing.

conceptual.question: What exception for arr[-1] or arr[arr.length]?
How does enhanced for (for-each) work with arrays?
conceptual.answer: It iterates elements without index; you cannot assign to the loop variable to change the array contents.

conceptual.question: What is two-pointer technique used for?
conceptual.answer: Often for sorted array problems: pair sum, palindrome check, removing duplicates in-place.

conceptual.question: What is Arrays.binarySearch precondition?
conceptual.answer: The array must be sorted (by natural order or comparator); otherwise the result is undefined.

conceptual.question: Difference between Arrays.equals and deepEquals?
conceptual.answer: equals compares one-dimensional content; deepEquals handles nested arrays recursively.

conceptual.question: Why might parallelSort help on large primitive arrays?
conceptual.answer: Arrays.parallelSort splits work across threads for big arrays; overhead can hurt small arrays.

codeBased.question: Write code to find maximum element in int[].
codeBased.answer: Track max = arr[0], loop from 1, update if arr[i] > max; handle empty array separately.

codeBased.question: Reverse an int[] in place.
codeBased.answer: Two pointers from both ends swap until meet.

codeBased.question: Copy first half of array to new array.
Check if array contains duplicate using a Set.
codeBased.answer: Add elements to HashSet; if add returns false, duplicate exists.

codeBased.question: Sum all elements with enhanced for.
codeBased.answer: int s=0; for(int x: arr) s+=x;

codeBased.question: Create 3x3 matrix and print row sums.
codeBased.answer: Nested loops, inner sums each row.

codeBased.question: Find index of first occurrence of target or -1.
codeBased.answer: Linear search loop with break or return index when found.

codeBased.question: Compare two int[] for equality.
codeBased.answer: Arrays.equals(a, b) for contents, not == for reference equality.

seniorScenario.question: Large byte[] allocation causes OOM in service. Mitigation?
seniorScenario.answer: Stream processing, chunking, or memory-mapped files instead of loading full array.

seniorScenario.question: Array-based cache grows unbounded. Fix?
seniorScenario.answer: Use bounded structures (ring buffer, LRU), eviction policy, and monitor heap.

seniorScenario.question: Parallel sort on huge primitive array—when beneficial?
seniorScenario.answer: Arrays.parallelSort for large arrays on multicore; measure overhead for small arrays.

seniorScenario.question: Off-by-one in production batch indexing caused wrong shard. Prevention?
seniorScenario.answer: Unit tests for boundary indices, assert invariants, and code review for loop bounds.

seniorScenario.question: Migrating from array to ArrayList in hot API—risks?
seniorScenario.answer: Boxing cost, iterator invalidation patterns differ; profile and test regression.

seniorScenario.question: Varargs method receives array—what happens to callers?
seniorScenario.answer: Compiler packs arguments into an array; avoid passing null as sole vararg (ambiguous) and be careful with performance for hot paths.

wrongAnswers: Array length can be changed after creation with array.resize().
arr[arr.length] is the last valid index.
Arrays.sort works the same for int[] and String[] without any comparator difference.
Enhanced for loop allows removing elements from array during iteration safely.
Two-dimensional arrays in Java are always rectangular.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Rule | Interview Tip | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Definition | Give 1-line meaning first | Keep answer structured | Avoid vague statements |
| Syntax | Show minimal working code | Explain output | Do not skip imports/signature |
| Edge Cases | Always test boundaries | Mention at least one | Most bugs live here |
| Debugging | Print intermediate states | Explain failure reason | Guessing wastes time |
| Quality | Prefer clear names | Talk about readability | Clever but unclear code fails review |
